question_text: An electric heater rated at 2000 W is switched on for 30 minutes. Calculate the energy transferred to the surroundings in joules and in kilowatt-hours.
correct_answer: Time = 30 minutes = 1800 seconds. Energy = Power × Time = 2000 × 1800 = 3,600,000 J (or 3.6 MJ). In kWh: 2 kW × 0.5 hours = 1 kWh.
explanation: Energy is calculated by multiplying power by time. With power in watts and time in seconds, the answer is in joules. To convert to kilowatt-hours, use kilowatts and hours.
